reasoning: The EU's importation of Russian gas is now physically constrained by the closure of three of the four major pipeline routes. With the halt of the Ukraine transit route in January 2025, the only remaining pipeline to Europe is TurkStream, which has a maximum nameplate capacity of 15.75 bcm/yr (approx. ~4 bcm/quarter). Meanwhile, Russian LNG exports to the EU were reported at around 14 bcm for the first eight months of 2025, which translates to an average of ~5.25 bcm/quarter. The combined maximum possible delivery under current flows is slightly under 10 bcm/quarter. Reaching the 15 bcm threshold in any single quarter of 2025 would require a massive ~5 bcm/quarter increase, which exceeds the physical and export capacity limits without the reinstatement of the Ukraine transit or Nord Stream pipelines. As such, the outcome is exceedingly unlikely. I am forecasting at the lowest allowable limit based on guidelines.
